name: wikipedia_article_scraper
title: Wikipedia Article Scraper
description: Search Wikipedia or fetch a specific article through the official MediaWiki API, returning titles, page IDs, canonical URLs, summaries or full extracts, categories, links, images, revision metadata, and Wikidata IDs.

seo.title: Wikipedia Article Scraper Tool for Summaries and Page Metadata
seo.description: Search Wikipedia or fetch exact articles with Better Fetch via the official MediaWiki API, including summaries, full extracts, categories, links, images, page IDs, URLs, revisions, and Wikidata IDs.
seo.intro: Wikipedia Article Scraper gives agents a polite, official-API route into Wikipedia content. Search by keyword or fetch an exact article title or URL, then receive compact rows with page title, page ID, canonical URL, language, summary or full plain-text extract, categories, internal links, thumbnail and original image URLs, revision metadata, page length, and Wikidata ID when available. It is designed for knowledge-base enrichment, RAG ingestion, entity research, article monitoring, SEO research, educational tools, and AI workflows that need citation-friendly Wikipedia context without scraping raw HTML.

useCases.title: Knowledge-base enrichment
useCases.description: Fetch canonical Wikipedia titles, summaries, page URLs, images, categories, and Wikidata IDs to enrich company, person, place, product, or concept records.

useCases.title: RAG context collection
useCases.description: Collect bounded plain-text article extracts and source URLs for retrieval pipelines, research assistants, documentation bots, and educational AI agents.

useCases.title: Topic discovery
useCases.description: Search Wikipedia by keyword and return top matching pages with concise extracts, categories, thumbnails, and links before deciding which pages to crawl more deeply.

faqs.question: Does Wikipedia Article Scraper scrape Wikipedia HTML pages?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 uses the official MediaWiki Action API through Better Fetch and sends an explicit tool user agent. It returns normalized JSON data instead of parsing rendered HTML.

faqs.question: Can it fetch full article text?
faqs.answer: Yes, set extract to full. To keep tool responses useful for agents and MCP clients, max_extract_chars bounds the returned plain-text extract.

faqs.question: Does it support non-English Wikipedia editions?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set language to a Wikipedia language subdomain such as en, de, fr, es, pt, or simple. Version 0.1 validates the language subdomain and calls that edition's MediaWiki API.

mode.description: Fetch one exact article page or search Wikipedia for matching pages.

page_title.description: Exact Wikipedia page title for page mode, e.g. Web scraping.

page_url.description: Wikipedia article URL for page mode, e.g. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_scraping.

language.description: Wikipedia language subdomain, e.g. en, de, fr, es, pt, or simple.

max_results.description: Maximum search results to return in search mode.

extract.description: Return the introductory extract or a bounded full article extract.

max_extract_chars.description: Maximum characters of plain-text extract to return per page.

include_categories.description: Include visible Wikipedia category names when available.

include_links.description: Include a bounded comma-separated list of internal page links.

query.description: Search query or page title used

extract_truncated.description: Whether extract was truncated by max_extract_chars

wikidata_id.description: Linked Wikidata entity ID when available

short_description.description: Short description page property when available

original_image_url.description: Original page image URL when available

last_revision_id.description: Latest revision ID reported by MediaWiki

page_length.description: Page length in bytes reported by MediaWiki
